title: Pseudognaphalium elegans
taxonomy: Pseudognaphalium elegans is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is classified within the genus Pseudognaphalium and the subfamily Asteroideae. The species is native to the western coast of North America, including the southwestern United States and parts of Mexico. Pseudognaphalium elegans is further classified as belonging to the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. The classification of Pseudognaphalium elegans reflects its botanical relationships and evolutionary history.

intro: Pseudognaphalium elegans, also known as western needle-fleabane, is an annual or biennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the western regions of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection.
